findings.finding: Veto Surface explicitly documents R7 manifest-divergence revert-and-replay path but is silent on the equivalent R12/R13-revert-post-Commit-3 heavyweight-rewind path; reader following Veto Surface as authoritative catalog won't know R13 failure at Task 10 also requires Commit 3 revert.
findings.evidence_quote: Tasks 9–10 abort behavior on R7 binding rule violations: spec R7 specifies that if F-10's research-partition re-validation diverges from the manifest, F-10 aborts AND the operator reverts Commit 3, refreshes the manifest, re-commits Commit 2, and replays from Task 9.

findings.finding: Reviewer claimed R5 sha256 cannot match post-commit because lifecycle/archive/ already exists from Commit 3; this premise is contradicted by git checkout's documented behavior — git stash + git checkout HEAD~1 restores the working tree to the target commit's state, so the dry-run at HEAD~1 (= Commit 2) reproduces the captured sha256.
findings.evidence_quote: By the time Verification runs, Commit 3 has already been created — the sweep (git mv loops, rm -rf lifecycle/feat-a/) is already committed.

findings.finding: git stash pop conflict scenario has no documented resolution path if the verification block leaves conflict markers in the tree post-Commit-3.
findings.evidence_quote: If the stash pop encounters a merge conflict... git stash pop exits non-zero and leaves conflict markers in the working tree.

findings.finding: R5 automated re-derivation conflates external-reviewer-in-clean-clone intent with in-process executor automation; spec R5 acceptance reads as an external reviewer step but the plan implements it as an inline post-commit sequence.
findings.evidence_quote: The spec's R5 acceptance criterion is written as a step an external reviewer performs in a clean environment.

findings.finding: Task 10's git mv research-archive loop has the same non-idempotency gap as Task 9; partial completion + R7 'refresh manifest' recovery legitimizes broken state.
findings.evidence_quote: Task 10's research sweep (git mv research/<slug>/ research/archive/<slug>/ for each manifest slug) has no partial-completion detection.

findings.finding: 111+ backlog discovery_source: research/<slug> frontmatter fields become dead paths post-F-10; /cortex-core:refine Discovery Bootstrap reads these as agent context, silently failing.
findings.reviewer_angle: F-10 silent breakage of research/<slug> citations
findings.evidence_quote: git grep shows 111 backlog/*.md lines with discovery_source: research/<slug> pointing to slugs swept by F-10.

findings.finding: ~30 in-flight lifecycle artifacts (research.md, spec.md, plan.md outside lifecycle/archive/) cite swept research slugs as escalation targets, DR references, and implementation guides — forward-operational instructions, not historical footnotes.
findings.reviewer_angle: F-10 silent breakage of research/<slug> citations
findings.evidence_quote: lifecycle/tighten-1b-plan-agent-prompt-*/spec.md:90 directs operators to 'Escalate to research/competing-plan-synthesis/research.md DR-3 Option 3'.

findings.finding: docs/sdk.md:9 hyperlink and docs/setup.md:388 prose citation will produce broken GitHub links post-F-10.
findings.reviewer_angle: F-10 silent breakage of research/<slug> citations
findings.evidence_quote: docs/sdk.md:9 contains a Markdown hyperlink [research/claude-code-sdk-usage/research.md](../research/claude-code-sdk-usage/research.md) that will 404 on GitHub after F-10.

findings.finding: Spec edge case 82 makes a false claim about R10 catching body-cited archived slugs — R10 only validates lifecycle/<slug> forms, not research/<slug>.
findings.reviewer_angle: F-10 silent breakage of research/<slug> citations
findings.evidence_quote: Backlog ticket body still cites a now-archived slug after the sweep: R10's integration test catches this; the rewrite recipe handled body-text and frontmatter spec:/discovery_source: fields.

findings.finding: Surviving research artifacts (competing-plan-synthesis, sandbox-overnight-child-agents, post-113-repo-state) contain cross-citations to swept research slugs that go unrewritten.
findings.reviewer_angle: F-10 silent breakage of research/<slug> citations
findings.evidence_quote: research/competing-plan-synthesis/research.md, research/sandbox-overnight-child-agents/research.md, research/post-113-repo-state/research.md... contain body-text citations to swept slugs.

findings.finding: events.log feature_complete schema does not include commit_sha field in any lifecycle dir; the 'use lifecycle artifact for SHA' alternative is not currently viable without a lifecycle skill change.
findings.evidence_quote: grep -r '"event": "feature_complete"' lifecycle/ shows ts/feature/tasks_total/rework_cycles fields only; no commit_sha field in any entry.

findings.finding: Task 8 abort guard does not fire for #168 even though #168 is in plan phase with no feature_complete event — c019e97 is returned and trivially passes ancestor check.
findings.evidence_quote: the abort condition does not fire — even though #168 is in plan phase with no feature-complete event.
